channel.title: Craft
channel.description: Craft is a list of hand oriented craft and technology Do we really need all those power tools?

item.title: Door Frames
item.description: The following is excerpted from “Doormaking & Window-Making,” and refers to frames for exterior doors. (The book instructs that interior “doors are usually hung in “jamb linings,” instead of door frames, these being fixed in the walls after the house is built.”) A handbook on doors would hardly be complete without some reference to door...
item.source:markdown: The following is excerpted from “Doormaking & Window-Making,” and refers to frames for exterior doors. (The book instructs that interior “doors are usually hung in “jamb linings,” instead of door frames, these being fixed in the walls after the house is built.”) A handbook on doors would hardly be complete without some reference to door...

item.title: Bye-Bye Batteries, Responsibly
item.description:
Cordless tools and the batteries that keep them humming are one of the biggest boons to woodworkers. But they eventually become obsolete. Unlike common trash, old batteries deserve the respect they’ve...

Source
item.source:markdown:

But they eventually become obsolete. Unlike common trash, old batteries deserve the respect they’ve...
[Source](https://www.popularwoodworking.com/editors-blog/bye-bye-batteries-responsibly/)

item.title: Spalted Maple
item.description:
What makes this wood so special? Fungus. If there’s one wood that can really challenge your sense of design—and your woodworking skills—it’s spalted maple. Those inky black lines can both...

Source

[Source](https://www.popularwoodworking.com/wood/spalted-maple/)

item.description:

item.title: Adirondack Loveseat
item.description:

item.title: Using Nails: The Basics
item.description: The following is excerpted from “Smalls: 18 Well-designed, Easy Projects for an Afternoon in the Workshop,” which is now available! In addition to the projects, you’ll find chapters on the techniques you need to build them (which will, of course, come in handy for all sorts of projects – not just the 18 in this...

In addition to the projects, you’ll find chapters on the techniques you need to build them (which will, of course, come in handy for all sorts of projects – not just the 18 in this...

item.title: Coming in 2027: A New Woodworking School in Covington
item.description: Thanks to some tectonic shifts in our business at Lost Art Press, it looks like we will have a new woodworking school in Covington, Kentucky, that is literally in our back yard. Last year we had to stop holding classes in our Willard Street workshop after our insurance carrier dumped us (it’s a long tale...
item.source:markdown: Thanks to some tectonic shifts in our business at Lost Art Press, it looks like we will have a new woodworking school in Covington, Kentucky, that is literally in our back yard. Last year we had to stop holding classes in our Willard Street workshop after our insurance carrier dumped us (it’s a long tale...

item.title: New Model Mandolin: 7
item.description:
Well, this is another long video but so, so much to get through!

The bridge is made and I have brief chat about its design, the strings go on and I show you how I set-up my mandolins. And finally……..the New Model Mandolin gets to sing its first notes!

So, cuppa at the ready and off we go.

Cheers Gary
item.source:markdown:
Well, this is another long video but so, so much to get through!
The bridge is made and I have brief chat about its design, the strings go on and I show you how I set-up my mandolins. And finally……..the New Model Mandolin gets to sing its first notes!
So, cuppa at the ready and off we go.
Cheers Gary
item.author: noreply@blogger.com (Gary Nava, Luthier)

item.title: Impossible Dovetails
item.description:
Baffle your friends with perplexing joints Press a dovetailed board into another board with matching sockets, and you’ve created woodworking’s most iconic joint. The dovetails and sockets wedge the boards...

item.title: 115. Marco Bortolozzo
item.description:
For Episode 115 of the podcast, we're joined by classical guitarmaker Marco Bortolozzo.

In our conversation, Marco tells us about the mentorship that launched his career, and the story behind his signature Olympic ring rosette, which was inspired by a 19th-century Spanish guitar. We also discuss his neck joint design, his unique use of shellac records in guitar finishes and much more.

https://www.marcobortolozzo.com/

Luthier on Luthier is hosted by Michael Bashkin of Bashkin Guitars and brought to you by the Fretboard Journal. This episode is sponsored by the Looth Group, Dream Guitars and StewMac.
item.source:markdown:
For Episode 115 of the podcast, we're joined by classical guitarmaker Marco Bortolozzo.
In our conversation, Marco tells us about the mentorship that launched his career, and the story behind his signature Olympic ring rosette, which was inspired by a 19th-century Spanish guitar. We also discuss his neck joint design, his unique use of shellac records in guitar finishes and much more.
[https://www.marcobortolozzo.com/](https://www.marcobortolozzo.com/)
Luthier on Luthier is hosted by Michael Bashkin of [Bashkin Guitars](https://www.bashkinguitars.com) and brought to you by the [Fretboard Journal](https://shop.fretboardjournal.com/products/fretboard-journal-annual-subscription). This episode is sponsored by [the Looth Group](https://loothgroup.com), [Dream Guitars](https://www.dreamguitars.com/) and [StewMac](https://www.stewmac.com/?irclickid=VA-TmuXZ%3AxyPUn0Ut-05ZTupUkHUPAzGE2bmy00&utm_source=3755630&utm_medium=Impact&utm_campaign=3755630&utm_content=Online%20Tracking%20Link_1303370&irgwc=1&partner=Fretboard%20Journal&mpid=3755630&group=).

item.title: LAP Storefront Closed on Friday, July 3
item.description: The Lost Art Press storefront, office and warehouse will be closed on Friday, July 3, 2026, for the Independence Day holiday. Normal store hours will resume on Monday. (And those are, Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday and Friday from 10 a.m.-2 p.m.)
item.source:markdown: The Lost Art Press storefront, office and warehouse will be closed on Friday, July 3, 2026, for the Independence Day holiday. Normal store hours will resume on Monday. (And those are, Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday and Friday from 10 a.m.-2 p.m.)

item.title: A Tailored Tea Table
item.description:
A graceful slipper-footed design enhanced with additional period details. In Colonial America, prior to Paul Revere’s famous midnight ride, colonists adopted many of the lifestyles of English citizens. One such...

item.title: Meet the Newest Editor: Katherine Schwarz
item.description: Katherine Schwarz was born in April 2001. She grew up in Fort Mitchell, Kentucky, with her sister, Maddy (five years older), her mom, Lucy May, and her dad, Christopher Schwarz. “Both of my parents have just been the best ever when it comes to raising us,” Katherine says. “My dad worked at the magazine...
item.source:markdown: Katherine Schwarz was born in April 2001. She grew up in Fort Mitchell, Kentucky, with her sister, Maddy (five years older), her mom, Lucy May, and her dad, Christopher Schwarz. “Both of my parents have just been the best ever when it comes to raising us,” Katherine says. “My dad worked at the magazine...

item.title: Free Plans for Our Clamshell Bookcase
item.description: I’ve always loved clamshell bookcases – fold-up bookcases that allow you to transport your personal library to a lighthouse, campsite or war zone. I started building them while working on my book “Campaign Furniture” (a free pdf download for everyone). They can be simple (such as the one shown above), or as complex as the...
item.source:markdown: I’ve always loved clamshell bookcases – fold-up bookcases that allow you to transport your personal library to a lighthouse, campsite or war zone. I started building them while working on my book “Campaign Furniture” (a free pdf download for everyone). They can be simple (such as the one shown above), or as complex as the...

item.title: New Model Mandolin: 6
item.description:
Time now to shape the neck!

Also, I have a brief chat about the tools needed for this process. After running through the shaping process, I start the clean-up in preparation for setting-up the mandolin, “in the white.”



Cheers Gary
item.source:markdown:
Time now to shape the neck!
Also, I have a brief chat about the tools needed for this process. After running through the shaping process, I start the clean-up in preparation for setting-up the mandolin, “in the white.”
Cheers Gary
item.author: noreply@blogger.com (Gary Nava, Luthier)
